title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: Market closes mixed on July 22, 2026, as Big Tech earnings loom

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Mixed as Energy Rises, Tech Slips Ahead of Key Earnings
items.summary: The U.S. stock market closed mixed on Wednesday, July 22, 2026, as investors cautiously awaited major earnings reports from tech giants like Alphabet and Tesla after the bell. Rising oil prices, fueled by escalating Middle East tensions, drove gains in the energy sector and kept inflation concerns at the forefront, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq saw a slight pullback.

items.headline: Super Micro Computer surges on strong AI order backlog.
items.summary: Super Micro Computer ($SMCI) soared +21.4% today after reporting over $60 billion in new orders for its fiscal fourth quarter and expecting higher gross margins, signaling robust demand in the AI server infrastructure market.

items.headline: AT&T delivers solid Q2 results, boosts subscriber additions.
items.summary: AT&T ($T) advanced today after announcing strong second-quarter results, with revenue up 2.3% to $31.6 billion and robust wireless subscriber additions that surpassed Wall Street's forecasts.

items.headline: Westinghouse Air Brake Technologies climbs on Q2 earnings beat.
items.summary: Westinghouse Air Brake Technologies ($WAB) rose +10.7% following a strong second-quarter earnings beat, coupled with higher full-year financial guidance and solid margin improvements.

items.headline: Wetour Robotics plunges amidst significant decline.
items.summary: Wetour Robotics Limited ($WETO) experienced a dramatic decline, falling -81.10% today to $0.151.

items.headline: China Pharma Holdings sees sharp drop.
items.summary: China Pharma Holdings, Inc. ($CPHI) fell significantly by -75.19% to $2.010 today, marking it as one of the biggest decliners.

items.headline: Pegasystems tumbles after Q2 earnings miss and cautious outlook.
items.summary: Pegasystems ($PEGA) dropped -16.7% after missing Wall Street's second-quarter earnings expectations, reporting slower annual contract growth, and warning of delays in AI-driven customer spending.

items.headline: Energy and Utilities Lead Gains Amid Oil Surge; Tech Under Pressure
items.summary: On Wednesday, Utilities, Energy, and Materials sectors led market gains, with Energy benefiting from a surge in oil prices to over $86 a barrel due to Middle East tensions. Conversely, the Technology and Communication Services sectors experienced declines as investors showed caution ahead of major tech earnings and concerns about AI-related spending continued.

items.headline: Inflation Concerns Persist as PPI Rises; Fed in Blackout Period
items.summary: The Producer Price Index (PPI) for final demand increased 5.5% over the year ended June 2026, contributing to ongoing inflation concerns in the market. The Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) is currently in a blackout period ahead of its July 28-29 meeting, having held its benchmark rate steady at 3.50–3.75% in June. Investors are anticipating potential rate adjustments later in the year, with at least one 25 basis point rate increase priced in for 2026.

items.headline: Big Tech Earnings in Focus: Alphabet and Tesla Report After Hours
items.summary: A busy earnings season continues with significant reports after today's market close from Alphabet ($GOOGL, $GOOG) and Tesla ($TSLA), with IBM ($IBM) also reporting. Earlier today, CME Group Inc. ($CME) announced strong Q2 financial results, and AT&T ($T) delivered robust Q2 performance. Market participants are closely watching these results, particularly for insights into AI-related investments and monetization.

items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions Drive Oil Prices Higher and Fuel Market Uncertainty
items.summary: Escalating hostilities in the Middle East, particularly the war involving Iran, continued to push crude oil prices higher, with WTI crude surpassing $86 a barrel and Brent crude touching $92. This geopolitical uncertainty is increasing inflation fears and contributing to market volatility, with analysts noting the mounting 'Iran war stagflation' premium.
